content.title: The Impact of Wilderness Tourism
content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲述了偏远地区旅游市场的繁荣，各国积极推广其“荒野”地区，吸引高消费的游客，但这些地区的生态和居民文化都是脆弱的，而且大部分人类活动，包括旅游，都受到明确的季节限制。', 'B': '当一个地方成为主要的旅游目的地后，对当地社区的影响是深远的。例如，当山地农民在几周内作为外国徒步旅行者的搬运工可以赚到比一年在田地里工作赚的钱还多时，很多农民放弃了农活，这样农活就留给了家庭的其他成员。在一些山区，这导致了农业产量的严重下降和当地饮食的改变，因为没有足够的劳动力来维护梯田、灌溉系统和农作物。结果是这些地区的许多人转向外部供应的大米和其他食物。\n在北极和沙漠社区，全年的生存传统上依赖于狩猎动物、捕鱼和在相对短的季节采集水果。然而，随着一些居民参与旅游业，他们没有时间去采集野生食物，这导致对购买食物和商店的依赖增加。旅游业并不总是这些变化的罪魁祸首。各种形式的工资劳动或政府救济往往会破坏传统的生存系统。无论原因如何，困境总是相同的：如果这些新的外部收入来源枯竭了会发生什么？\n游客的物理影响是冒险旅游增长所带来的另一个严重问题。人们关注的焦点主要集中在主要步道的侵蚀问题上，但更重要的可能是由于需要为游客提供烹饪食物和热水淋浴而导致的森林砍伐和对水资源的影响。在山区和沙漠中，生长缓慢的树木通常是燃料的主要来源，而水资源可能有限或容易因过度使用而受到破坏。', 'C': '旅游问题的故事在过去几年中变得司空见惯，然而这并不一定是个问题。尽管旅游不可避免地影响到所在地区，但对这些脆弱环境和当地文化的成本可以最小化，甚至可以成为振兴当地文化的工具。越来越多的冒险旅游经营者正努力确保他们的活动对当地人口和环境的长期利益。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text:
The market for tourism in remote areas is booming as never before. Countries all across the world are actively promoting their 'wilderness' regions - such as mountains, Arctic lands, deserts, small islands and wetlands - to high-spending tourists. The attraction of these areas is obvious: by definition, wilderness tourism requires little or no initial investment. But that does not mean that there is no cost. As the 1992 United Nations Conference on Environment and Development recognized, these regions are fragile (i.e. highly vulnerable to abnormal pressures) not just in terms of their ecology, but also in terms of the culture of their inhabitants. The three most significant types of fragile environment in these respects, and also in terms of the proportion of the Earth's surface they cover, are deserts, mountains and Arctic areas. An important characteristic is their marked seasonality, with harsh conditions prevailing for many months each year. Consequently, most human activities, including tourism, are limited to quite clearly defined parts of the year.
Tourists are drawn to these regions by their natural landscape beauty and the unique cultures of their indigenous people. And poor governments in these isolated areas have welcomed the new breed of 'adventure tourist', grateful for the hard currency they bring. For several years now, tourism has been the prime source of foreign exchange in Nepal and Bhutan. Tourism is also a key element in the economies of Arctic zones such as Lapland and Alaska and in desert areas such as Ayers Rock in Australia and Arizona's Monument Valley.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text:
Once a location is established as a main tourist destination, the effects on the local community are profound. When hill-farmers, for example, can make more money in a few weeks working as porters for foreign trekkers than they can in a year working in their fields, it is not surprising that many of them give up their farm-work, which is thus left to other members of the family. In some hill-regions, this has led to a serious decline in farm output and a change in the local diet, because there is insufficient labour to maintain terraces and irrigation systems and tend to crops. The result has been that many people in these regions have turned to outside supplies of rice and other foods.
In Arctic and desert societies, year-round survival has traditionally depended on hunting animals and fish and collecting fruit over a relatively short season. However, as some inhabitants become involved in tourism, they no longer have time to collect wild food; this has led to increasing dependence on bought food and stores. Tourism is not always the culprit behind such changes. All kinds of wage labour, or government handouts, tend to undermine traditional survival systems. Whatever the cause, the dilemma is always the same: what happens if these new, external sources of income dry up?
The physical impact of visitors is another serious problem associated with the growth in adventure tourism. Much attention has focused on erosion along major trails, but perhaps more important are the deforestation and impacts on water supplies arising from the need to provide tourists with cooked food and hot showers. In both mountains and deserts, slow-growing trees are often the main sources of fuel and water supplies may be limited or vulnerable to degradation through heavy use.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text:
Stories about the problems of tourism have become legion in the last few years. Yet it does not have to be a problem. Although tourism inevitably affects the region in which it takes place, the costs to these fragile environments and their local cultures can be minimized. Indeed, it can even be a vehicle for reinvigorating local cultures, as has happened with the Sherpas of Nepal's Khumbu Valley and in some Alpine villages. And a growing number of adventure tourism operators are trying to ensure that their activities benefit the local population and environment over the long term.
In the Swiss Alps, communities have decided that their future depends on integrating tourism more effectively with the local economy. Local concern about the rising number of second home developments in the Swiss Pays d'Enhaut resulted in limits being imposed on their growth. There has also been a renaissance in communal cheese production in the area, providing the locals with a reliable source of income that does not depend on outside visitors.
Many of the Arctic tourist destinations have been exploited by outside companies, who employ transient workers and repatriate most of the profits to their home base. But some Arctic communities are now operating tour businesses themselves, thereby ensuring that the benefits accrue locally. For instance, a native corporation in Alaska, employing local people, is running an air tour from Anchorage to Kotzebue, where tourists eat Arctic food, walk on the tundra and watch local musicians and dancers.
Native people in the desert regions of the American Southwest have followed similar strategies, encouraging tourists to visit their pueblos and reservations to purchase high-quality handicrafts and artwork. The Acoma and San Ildefonso pueblos have established highly profitable pottery businesses, while the Navajo and Hopi groups have been similarly successful with jewellery.
Too many people living in fragile environments have lost control over their economies, their culture and their environment when tourism has penetrated their homelands. Merely restricting tourism cannot be the solution to the imbalance, because people's desire to see new places will not just disappear. Instead, communities in fragile environments must achieve greater control over tourism ventures in their regions, in order to balance their needs and aspirations with the demands of tourism. A growing number of communities are demonstrating that, with firm communal decision-making, this is possible. The critical question now is whether this can become the norm, rather than the exception.

groups.title: List of Headings
groups.desc:
Questions 1-3

Reading Passage 1 has three paragraphs, A-C.
Choose the correct heading for each section and move it into the gap.

list.text: The expansion of international tourism in recent years
How local communities can balance their own needs with the demands of wilderness tourism
Fragile regions and the reasons for the expansion of tourism there
Traditional methods of food-supply in fragile regions
Some of the disruptive effects of wilderness tourism
The economic benefits of mass tourism

questions.analysis: Section A主要讨论了脆弱地区（如山脉、北极地区、沙漠等）旅游业的扩展及其原因。文章提到这些地区的旅游市场正在快速增长，并且各国都在积极推广这些地区以吸引游客。这与小标题iii: Fragile regions and the reasons for the expansion of tourism there完全吻合。

central_sentences.sentence: Countries all across the world are actively promoting their 'wilderness' regions - such as mountains, Arctic lands, deserts, small islands and wetlands - to high-spending tourists.
central_sentences.translation: 世界各国都在积极向高消费游客推广他们的"荒野"地区——如山区、北极地区、沙漠、小岛和湿地。

target_sen.target_sen: Countries all across the world are actively promoting their 'wilderness' regions - such as mountains, Arctic lands, deserts, small islands and wetlands - to high-spending tourists.
The market for tourism in remote areas is booming as never before.
The market for tourism in remote areas is booming as never before.
Countries all across the world are actively promoting their 'wilderness' regions - such as mountains, Arctic lands, deserts, small islands and wetlands - to high-spending tourists.

questions.analysis: Section B主要讨论了旅游对当地社区的负面影响，包括农产品产量下降、饮食变化以及对外部食品供应的依赖增加。这些内容都指向了旅游对当地社区的破坏性影响，即“disruptive effects”。因此，Section B对应的小标题是v: Some of the disruptive effects of wilderness tourism。

central_sentences.sentence: Once a location is established as a main tourist destination, the effects on the local community are profound.
central_sentences.translation: 一旦一个地方成为主要的旅游目的地，对当地社区的影响就会很深远。

syn_ary.synonym: effects on the local community are profound

target_sen.target_sen: Once a location is established as a main tourist destination, the effects on the local community are profound.

In some hill-regions, this has led to a serious decline in farm output and a change in the local diet, because there is insufficient labour to maintain terraces and irrigation systems and tend to crops.

In some hill-regions, this has led to a serious decline in farm output and a change in the local diet, because there is insufficient labour to maintain terraces and irrigation systems and tend to crops.

syn_ary.synonym: increasing dependence on bought food and stores

target_sen.target_sen: However, as some inhabitants become involved in tourism, they no longer have time to collect wild food; this has led to increasing dependence on bought food and stores.
However, as some inhabitants become involved in tourism, they no longer have time to collect wild food; this has led to increasing dependence on bought food and stores.
The physical impact of visitors is another serious problem associated with the growth in adventure tourism.

When hill-farmers, for example, can make more money in a few weeks working as porters for foreign trekkers than they can in a year working in their fields, it is not surprising that many of them give up their farm-work, which is thus left to other members of the family.

questions.analysis: Section C 主要讨论了当地社区如何通过有效的策略来平衡自身需求和旅游业需求。这与小标题 ii: How local communities can balance their own needs with the demands of wilderness tourism 的内容最为贴合。段落中提到的“integrating tourism more effectively with the local economy” 和“ensuring that their activities benefit the local population and environment over the long term” 都表明了当地社区在平衡旅游需求和自身需求方面的努力。

central_sentences.sentence: In the Swiss Alps, communities have decided that their future depends on integrating tourism more effectively with the local economy.
central_sentences.translation: 在瑞士阿尔卑斯山，社区已经决定他们的未来取决于将旅游业更有效地融入当地经济。

target_sen.target_sen: In the Swiss Alps, communities have decided that their future depends on integrating tourism more effectively with the local economy.
In the Swiss Alps, communities have decided that their future depends on integrating tourism more effectively with the local economy.
But some Arctic communities are now operating tour businesses themselves, thereby ensuring that the benefits accrue locally.
Instead, communities in fragile environments must achieve greater control over tourism ventures in their regions, in order to balance their needs and aspirations with the demands of tourism.
A growing number of communities are demonstrating that, with firm communal decision-making, this is possible.
In the Swiss Alps, communities have decided that their future depends on integrating tourism more effectively with the local economy.
Stories about the problems of tourism have become legion in the last few years.
Although tourism inevitably affects the region in which it takes place, the costs to these fragile environments and their local cultures can be minimized.
And a growing number of adventure tourism operators are trying to ensure that their activities benefit the local population and environment over the long term.
In the Swiss Alps, communities have decided that their future depends on integrating tourism more effectively with the local economy.
Too many people living in fragile environments have lost control over their economies, their culture and their environment when tourism has penetrated their homelands.
Merely restricting tourism cannot be the solution to the imbalance, because people's desire to see new places will not just disappear.
Instead, communities in fragile environments must achieve greater control over tourism ventures in their regions, in order to balance their needs and aspirations with the demands of tourism.

groups.desc:
Questions 4-9

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 1?
Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer,
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer,
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

questions.title: The low financial cost of setting up wilderness tourism makes it attractive to many countries.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“low financial cost”（低财务成本）在原文中对应“little or no initial investment”（几乎没有初始投资），而“attractive”（有吸引力的）对应“attraction”（吸引力）。原文明确指出，荒野旅游的吸引力在于其几乎不需要初始投资，这与题干的意思完全一致。因此，答案是YES。

central_sentences.sentence: The attraction of these areas is obvious: by definition, wilderness tourism requires little or no initial investment.
central_sentences.translation: 这些地区的吸引力是显而易见的：根据定义，荒野旅游几乎不需要或只需要很少的初始投资。

target_sen.target_sen: The attraction of these areas is obvious: by definition, wilderness tourism requires little or no initial investment.
The attraction of these areas is obvious: by definition, wilderness tourism requires little or no initial investment.
The attraction of these areas is obvious: by definition, wilderness tourism requires little or no initial investment.

questions.title: Deserts, mountains and Arctic regions are examples of environments that are both ecologically and culturally fragile.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "Deserts, mountains and Arctic regions are examples of environments that are both ecologically and culturally fragile" 在文章中找到了明确的对应表达。文章中提到 "deserts, mountains and Arctic areas" 以及 "fragile... not just in terms of their ecology, but also in terms of the culture" 与题干中的描述完全一致。因此，答案是 YES。

central_sentences.sentence: As the 1992 United Nations Conference on Environment and Development recognized, these regions are fragile (i.e. highly vulnerable to abnormal pressures) not just in terms of their ecology, but also in terms of the culture of their inhabitants. The three most significant types of fragile environment in these respects, and also in terms of the proportion of the Earth's surface they cover, are deserts, mountains and Arctic areas.
central_sentences.translation: 正如1992年联合国环境与发展会议所认识到的，这些地区不仅在生态方面，而且在居民文化方面都是脆弱的（即高度易受异常压力影响）。在这些方面，以及在它们覆盖地球表面的比例方面，三种最重要的脆弱环境类型是沙漠、山区和北极地区。

target_sen.target_sen: The three most significant types of fragile environment in these respects, and also in terms of the proportion of the Earth's surface they cover, are deserts, mountains and Arctic areas.

questions.title: Wilderness tourism operates throughout the year in fragile areas.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“wilderness tourism operates throughout the year in fragile areas”意思是荒野旅游在脆弱区域全年运营。而原文中明确提到这些区域的旅游活动受季节性影响，仅限于一年中特定的时间段（limited to quite clearly defined parts of the year），这与题干中的“throughout the year”相矛盾。因此，正确答案是NO。

central_sentences.sentence: Consequently, most human activities, including tourism, are limited to quite clearly defined parts of the year.
central_sentences.translation: 因此，包括旅游在内的大多数人类活动仅限于一年中明确定义的某些时期。

target_sen.target_sen: The market for tourism in remote areas is booming as never before.
The attraction of these areas is obvious: by definition, wilderness tourism requires little or no initial investment.
Consequently, most human activities, including tourism, are limited to quite clearly defined parts of the year.
For several years now, tourism has been the prime source of foreign exchange in Nepal and Bhutan.

syn_ary.synonym: limited to quite clearly defined parts of the year

target_sen.target_sen: Consequently, most human activities, including tourism, are limited to quite clearly defined parts of the year.
The three most significant types of fragile environment in these respects, and also in terms of the proportion of the Earth's surface they cover, are deserts, mountains and Arctic areas.

questions.title: The spread of tourism in certain hill-regions has resulted in a fall in the amount of food produced locally.
questions.analysis: 答案是YES，因为题干中的信息在原文中得到了明确的对应和支持。题干提到旅游业的扩展导致某些山地区域的当地食品产量下降，原文中明确提到在一些山地区域，由于旅游业的发展，导致农产品产量严重下降。这些信息在原文中有直接的同义替换，确认了题干的陈述。

central_sentences.sentence: In some hill-regions, this has led to a serious decline in farm output and a change in the local diet, because there is insufficient labour to maintain terraces and irrigation systems and tend to crops.
central_sentences.translation: 在一些山区，这导致了农业产出的严重下降和当地饮食的改变，因为没有足够的劳动力来维护梯田和灌溉系统以及照料农作物。

target_sen.target_sen: In some hill-regions, this has led to a serious decline in farm output and a change in the local diet, because there is insufficient labour to maintain terraces and irrigation systems and tend to crops.

In some hill-regions, this has led to a serious decline in farm output and a change in the local diet, because there is insufficient labour to maintain terraces and irrigation systems and tend to crops.

locating_words.locating_word: fall in the amount of food produced locally

questions.title: Traditional food-gathering in desert societies was distributed evenly over the year.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Traditional food-gathering in desert societies was distributed evenly over the year”意思是“沙漠社会的传统食物采集是全年均匀分布的”。而原文中提到“collecting fruit over a relatively short season”，意思是“在相对较短的季节内采集水果”，这与题干的描述相反。因此，正确答案是NO，即题干的描述与文章内容相反。

central_sentences.sentence: In Arctic and desert societies, year-round survival has traditionally depended on hunting animals and fish and collecting fruit over a relatively short season.
central_sentences.translation: 在北极和沙漠社会，全年的生存传统上依赖于在相对较短的季节内狩猎动物和鱼类以及采集水果。

target_sen.target_sen: In Arctic and desert societies, year-round survival has traditionally depended on hunting animals and fish and collecting fruit over a relatively short season.

syn_ary.synonym: hunting animals and fish and collecting fruit

target_sen.target_sen: In Arctic and desert societies, year-round survival has traditionally depended on hunting animals and fish and collecting fruit over a relatively short season.
In Arctic and desert societies, year-round survival has traditionally depended on hunting animals and fish and collecting fruit over a relatively short season.

questions.title: Government handouts do more damage than tourism does to traditional patterns of food-gathering.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息要求比较“政府补助”和“旅游”对传统食物采集模式的影响大小。虽然原文提到“旅游”和“政府补助”都会破坏传统的生存系统，但并没有明确比较两者的影响大小。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到直接的对应，正确答案是“NOT GIVEN”。

central_sentences.sentence: Tourism is not always the culprit behind such changes. All kinds of wage labour, or government handouts, tend to undermine traditional survival systems. Whatever the cause, the dilemma is always the same: what happens if these new, external sources of income dry up?
central_sentences.translation: 旅游并不总是这些变化背后的罪魁祸首。各种形式的工资劳动或政府补助都倾向于破坏传统的生存系统。无论原因是什么，困境总是相同的：如果这些新的、外部的收入来源枯竭了，会发生什么？

target_sen.target_sen: All kinds of wage labour, or government handouts, tend to undermine traditional survival systems.
However, as some inhabitants become involved in tourism, they no longer have time to collect wild food; this has led to increasing dependence on bought food and stores.
The physical impact of visitors is another serious problem associated with the growth in adventure tourism.
All kinds of wage labour, or government handouts, tend to undermine traditional survival systems.

groups.title: The positive ways in which some local communities have responded to tourism

Questions 10-13
Complete the table below.
Write ONE WORD from the text in each gap.

questions.analysis: 答案是“cheese”，因为题干中的“Revived production”在原文中对应的是“renaissance in production”，而“Swiss Pays d'Enhaut”在原文中也明确提到。原文句子提到“renaissance in communal cheese production”，即奶酪生产的复兴，因此答案是“cheese”。

central_sentences.sentence: There has also been a renaissance in communal cheese production in the area, providing the locals with a reliable source of income that does not depend on outside visitors.
central_sentences.translation: 该地区的社区奶酪生产也出现了复兴，为当地人提供了一个不依赖外来游客的可靠收入来源。

target_sen.target_sen: There has also been a renaissance in communal cheese production in the area, providing the locals with a reliable source of income that does not depend on outside visitors.

questions.analysis: 答案是“tour”，因为题干中的“Operate 【 】businesses”与原文中的“operating tour businesses themselves”直接对应。题干中的“Operate”对应原文中的“operating”，“businesses”对应原文中的“tour businesses”。因此，正确答案是“tour”。

central_sentences.sentence: But some Arctic communities are now operating tour businesses themselves, thereby ensuring that the benefits accrue locally.
central_sentences.translation: 但是现在一些北极社区正在自己经营旅游业务，从而确保利益能够留在当地。

The Acoma and San Ildefonso pueblos have established highly profitable pottery businesses, while the Navajo and Hopi groups have been similarly successful with jewellery.
But some Arctic communities are now operating tour businesses themselves, thereby ensuring that the benefits accrue locally.

questions.analysis: 答案是"pottery"。题目要求填入Acoma和San Ildefonso的生产和销售的产品。原文明确提到"Acoma and San Ildefonso pueblos have established highly profitable pottery businesses"，这表明他们生产和销售陶器。因此，正确答案是"pottery"。

The Acoma and San Ildefonso pueblos have established highly profitable pottery businesses, while the Navajo and Hopi groups have been similarly successful with jewellery.

questions.analysis: 答案是“jewellery/jewelry”，因为在原文中明确提到“Navajo and Hopi groups have been similarly successful with jewellery”，这与题目中的“Produce and sell”直接对应。题目要求填入的是 Navajo 和 Hopi 族群生产和销售的产品，根据原文，这个产品是珠宝（jewellery/jewelry）。

central_sentences.sentence: the Navajo and Hopi groups have been similarly successful with jewellery.
central_sentences.translation: 纳瓦霍族和霍皮族在珠宝方面也取得了类似的成功。

target_sen.target_sen: The Acoma and San Ildefonso pueblos have established highly profitable pottery businesses, while the Navajo and Hopi groups have been similarly successful with jewellery.
The Acoma and San Ildefonso pueblos have established highly profitable pottery businesses, while the Navajo and Hopi groups have been similarly successful with jewellery.

content.title: Flawed Beauty: The Problem with Toughened Glass

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: On 2nd August 1999, a particularly hot day in the town of Cirencester in the UK, a large pane of toughened glass in the roof of a shopping centre at Bishops Walk shattered without warning and fell from its frame. When fragments were analysed by experts at the giant glass manufacturer Pilkington, which had made the pane, they found that minute crystals of nickel sulphide trapped inside the glass had almost certainly caused the failure.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: 'The glass industry is aware of the issue,' says Brian Waldron, chairman of the standards committee at the Glass and Glazing Federation, a British trade association, and standards development officer at Pilkington. But he insists that cases are few and far between. 'It's a very rare phenomenon,' he says.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Others disagree. 'On average I see about one or two buildings a month suffering from nickel sulphide related failures,' says Barrie Josie, a consultant engineer involved in the Bishops Walk investigation. Other experts tell of similar experiences. Tony Wilmott of London-based consulting engineers Sandberg, and Simon Armstrong at CladTech Associates in Hampshire both say they know of hundreds of cases. 'What you hear is only the tip of the iceberg,' says Trevor Ford, a glass expert at Resolve Engineering in Brisbane, Queensland. He believes the reason is simple: 'No-one wants bad press.'

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Toughened glass is found everywhere, from cars and bus shelters to the windows, walls and roofs of thousands of buildings around the world. It's easy to see why. This glass has five times the strength of standard glass, and when it does break it shatters into tiny cubes rather than large, razor-sharp shards. Architects love it because large panels can be bolted together to make transparent walls, and turning it into ceilings and floors is almost as easy.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: It is made by heating a sheet of ordinary glass to about 620°C to soften it slightly, allowing its structure to expand, and then cooling it rapidly with jets of cold air. This causes the outer layer of the pane to contract and solidify before the interior. When the interior finally solidifies and shrinks, it exerts a pull on the outer layer that leaves it in permanent compression and produces a tensile force inside the glass. As cracks propagate best in materials under tension, the compressive force on the surface must be overcome before the pane will break, making it more resistant to cracking.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The problem starts when glass contains nickel sulphide impurities. Trace amounts of nickel and sulphur are usually present in the raw materials used to make glass, and nickel can also be introduced by fragments of nickel alloys falling into the molten glass. As the glass is heated, these atoms react to form tiny crystals of nickel sulphide. Just a tenth of a gram of nickel in the furnace can create up to 50,000 crystals.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: These crystals can exist in two forms: a dense form called the alpha phase, which is stable at high temperatures, and a less dense form called the beta phase, which is stable at room temperatures. The high temperatures used in the toughening process convert all the crystals to the dense, compact alpha form. But the subsequent cooling is so rapid that the crystals don't have time to change back to the beta phase. This leaves unstable alpha crystals in the glass, primed like a coiled spring, ready to revert to the beta phase without warning.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: When this happens, the crystals expand by up to 4%. And if they are within the central, tensile region of the pane, the stresses this unleashes can shatter the whole sheet. The time that elapses before failure occurs is unpredictable. It could happen just months after manufacture, or decades later, although if the glass is heated - by sunlight, for example - the process is speeded up. Ironically, says Graham Dodd, of consulting engineers Arup in London, the oldest pane of toughened glass known to have failed due to nickel sulphide inclusions was in Pilkington's glass research building in Lathom, Lancashire. The pane was 27 years old.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Data showing the scale of the nickel sulphide problem is almost impossible to find. The picture is made more complicated by the fact that these crystals occur in batches. So even if, on average, there is only one inclusion in 7 tonnes of glass, if you experience one nickel sulphide failure in your building, that probably means you've got a problem in more than one pane. Josie says that in the last decade he has worked on over 15 buildings with the number of failures into double figures.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: One of the worst examples of this is Waterfront Place, which was completed in 1990. Over the following decade the 40-storey Brisbane block suffered a rash of failures. Eighty panes of its toughened glass shattered due to inclusions before experts were finally called in. John Barry, an expert in nickel sulphide contamination at the University of Queensland, analysed every glass pane in the building. Using a studio camera, a photographer went up in a cradle to take photos of every pane. These were scanned under a modified microfiche reader for signs of nickel sulphide crystals. 'We discovered at least another 120 panes with potentially dangerous inclusions which were then replaced,' says Barry. 'It was a very expensive and time-consuming process that took around six months to complete.' Though the project cost A$1.6 million (nearly £700,000), the alternative - re-cladding the entire building - would have cost ten times as much.

content.title: The effects of light on plant and animal species

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Light is important to organisms for two different reasons. Firstly it is used as a cue for the timing of daily and seasonal rhythms in both plants and animals, and secondly it is used to assist growth in plants.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Breeding in most organisms occurs during a part of the year only, and so a reliable cue is needed to trigger breeding behaviour. Day length is an excellent cue, because it provides a perfectly predictable pattern of change within the year. In the temperate zone in spring, temperatures fluctuate greatly from day to day, but day length increases steadily by a predictable amount. The seasonal impact of day length on physiological responses is called photoperiodism, and the amount of experimental evidence for this phenomenon is considerable. For example, some species of birds' breeding can be induced even in midwinter simply by increasing day length artificially (Wolfson 1964). Other examples of photoperiodism occur in plants. A short-day plant flowers when the day is less than a certain critical length. A long-day plant flowers after a certain critical day length is exceeded. In both cases the critical day length differs from species to species. Plants which flower after a period of vegetative growth, regardless of photoperiod, are known as day-neutral plants.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Breeding seasons in animals such as birds have evolved to occupy the part of the year in which offspring have the greatest chances of survival. Before the breeding season begins, food reserves must be built up to support the energy cost of reproduction, and to provide for young birds both when they are in the nest and after fledging. Thus many temperate-zone birds use the increasing day lengths in spring as a cue to begin the nesting cycle, because this is a point when adequate food resources will be assured.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The adaptive significance of photoperiodism in plants is also clear. Short-day plants that flower in spring in the temperate zone are adapted to maximising seedling growth during the growing season. Long-day plants are adapted for situations that require fertilization by insects, or a long period of seed ripening. Short-day plants that flower in the autumn in the temperate zone are able to build up food reserves over the growing season and over winter as seeds. Day-neutral plants have an evolutionary advantage when the connection between the favourable period for reproduction and day length is much less certain. For example, desert annuals germinate, flower and seed whenever suitable rainfall occurs, regardless of the day length.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The breeding season of some plants can be delayed to extraordinary lengths. Bamboos are perennial grasses that remain in a vegetative state for many years and then suddenly flower, fruit and die (Evans 1976). Every bamboo of the species Chusquea abietifolia on the island of Jamaica flowered, set seed and died during 1884. The next generation of bamboo flowered and died between 1916 and 1918, which suggests a vegetative cycle of about 31 years. The climatic trigger for this flowering cycle is not yet known, but the adaptive significance is clear. The simultaneous production of masses of bamboo seeds (in some cases lying 12 to 15 centimetres deep on the ground) is more than all the seed-eating animals can cope with at the time, so that some seeds escape being eaten and grow up to form the next generation (Evans 1976).

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The second reason light is important to organisms is that it is essential for photosynthesis. This is the process by which plants use energy from the sun to convert carbon from soil or water into organic material for growth. The rate of photosynthesis in a plant can be measured by calculating the rate of its uptake of carbon. There is a wide range of photosynthetic responses of plants to variations in light intensity. Some plants reach maximal photosynthesis at one-quarter full sunlight, and others, like sugarcane, never reach a maximum, but continue to increase photosynthesis rate as light intensity rises.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Plants in general can be divided into two groups: shade-tolerant species and shade-intolerant species. This classification is commonly used in forestry and horticulture. Shade-tolerant plants have lower photosynthetic rates and hence have lower growth rates than those of shade-intolerant species. Plant species become adapted to living in a certain kind of habitat, and in the process evolve a series of characteristics that prevent them from occupying other habitats. Grime (1966) suggests that light may be one of the major components directing these adaptations. For example, eastern hemlock seedlings are shade-tolerant. They can survive in the forest understorey under very low light levels because they have a low photosynthetic rate.

central_sentences.sentence: The seasonal impact of day length on physiological responses is called photoperiodism, and the amount of experimental evidence for this phenomenon is considerable.

For example, some species of birds' breeding can be induced even in midwinter simply by increasing day length artificially (Wolfson 1964).

questions.title: Photoperiodism is restricted to certain geographic areas.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“Photoperiodism”、“restricted”和“geographic areas”在原文中都有对应的词语，但原文并没有提到光周期现象是否仅限于某些地理区域。原文只是描述了光周期现象的定义和一些例子，但没有明确说明这种现象是否限制在某些地理区域。因此，题干中的信息在原文中找不到明确的支持或反驳，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: The seasonal impact of day length on physiological responses is called photoperiodism, and the amount of experimental evidence for this phenomenon is considerable.
central_sentences.translation: 光照时长对生理反应的季节性影响被称为光周期现象，而且有大量的实验证据支持这一现象。

target_sen.target_sen: The seasonal impact of day length on physiological responses is called photoperiodism, and the amount of experimental evidence for this phenomenon is considerable.
Other examples of photoperiodism occur in plants.
In the temperate zone in spring, temperatures fluctuate greatly from day to day, but day length increases steadily by a predictable amount.

questions.title: Desert annuals are examples of long-day plants.
questions.analysis: 题干中的陈述是“Desert annuals are examples of long-day plants.”，意思是沙漠一年生植物是长日植物的例子。而原文中明确提到沙漠一年生植物不受日长影响（regardless of the day length），这与长日植物的定义相反。因此，题干中的陈述与原文内容相反，所以答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: desert annuals germinate, flower and seed whenever suitable rainfall occurs, regardless of the day length
central_sentences.translation: 沙漠一年生植物在适宜的降雨条件下发芽、开花和结种，不受日长影响。

target_sen.target_sen: For example, desert annuals germinate, flower and seed whenever suitable rainfall occurs, regardless of the day length.
Long-day plants are adapted for situations that require fertilization by insects, or a long period of seed ripening.

questions.title: Bamboos flower several times during their life cycle.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Bamboos flower several times during their life cycle" 意思是竹子在其生命周期内多次开花。而原文中明确指出竹子在多年生长后只开一次花，然后结果并死亡。这与题干中的 "several times" 明显矛盾，因此正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Bamboos are perennial grasses that remain in a vegetative state for many years and then suddenly flower, fruit and die (Evans 1976).
central_sentences.translation: 竹子是多年生草本植物，它们在生长状态下维持多年，然后突然开花、结果并死亡（Evans 1976）。

target_sen.target_sen: Bamboos are perennial grasses that remain in a vegetative state for many years and then suddenly flower, fruit and die (Evans 1976).
Bamboos are perennial grasses that remain in a vegetative state for many years and then suddenly flower, fruit and die (Evans 1976).

questions.title: Scientists have yet to determine the cue for Chusquea abietifolia's seasonal rhythm.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "Scientists have yet to determine the cue for Chusquea abietifolia's seasonal rhythm" 可以在文章中找到对应的表达 "The climatic trigger for this flowering cycle is not yet known"。通过对比，"determine" 对应 "not yet known"，"cue" 对应 "climatic trigger"，"seasonal rhythm" 对应 "flowering cycle"。因此，题干信息在文章中得到了明确的体现，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: The climatic trigger for this flowering cycle is not yet known, but the adaptive significance is clear.
central_sentences.translation: 这种开花周期的气候触发因素尚未知晓，但其适应意义是明确的。

target_sen.target_sen: The climatic trigger for this flowering cycle is not yet known, but the adaptive significance is clear.
The climatic trigger for this flowering cycle is not yet known, but the adaptive significance is clear.
Every bamboo of the species Chusquea abietifolia on the island of Jamaica flowered, set seed and died during 1884.
The climatic trigger for this flowering cycle is not yet known, but the adaptive significance is clear.

questions.title: Eastern hemlock is a fast-growing plant.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Eastern hemlock is a fast-growing plant”意思是“东部铁杉是一种快速生长的植物”。在原文中，提到“Eastern hemlock seedlings are shade-tolerant. They can survive in the forest understorey under very low light levels because they have a low photosynthetic rate.”，意思是“东部铁杉幼苗是耐阴的。它们可以在森林的下层在非常低的光照水平下生存，因为它们的光合作用率很低。”。低光合作用率意味着生长速度较慢，因此题干中的“fast-growing”与原文中的“low photosynthetic rate”是相反的。所以，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: eastern hemlock seedlings are shade-tolerant. They can survive in the forest understorey under very low light levels because they have a low photosynthetic rate
central_sentences.translation: 东部铁杉幼苗是耐阴的。它们能在森林下层极低光照水平下生存，因为它们具有低光合作用率。

target_sen.target_sen: For example, eastern hemlock seedlings are shade-tolerant.
They can survive in the forest understorey under very low light levels because they have a low photosynthetic rate.

groups.desc:
Questions 34-40
Complete the sentences.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS from the text in each gap.
groups.collect:
Day length is a useful cue for breeding in areas where $2171$are unpredictable.
Plants which do not respond to light levels are referred to as $2172$.
Birds in temperate climates associate longer days with nesting and the availability of $2173$.
Plants that flower when days are long often depend on $2174$to help them reproduce.
Desert annuals respond to $2175$as a signal for reproduction.
There is no limit to the photosynthetic rate in plants such as $2176$.
Tolerance to shade is one criterion for the $2177$of plants in forestry and horticulture.

questions.analysis: 题干中的"Day length"和"unpredictable"在原文中分别对应"day length"和"fluctuate greatly"。原文提到在温带地区，温度变化很大（不可预测），而日长是稳定的信号。因此，题干中提到的"unpredictable"因素是温度，正确答案是"temperatures"。

central_sentences.sentence: In the temperate zone in spring, temperatures fluctuate greatly from day to day, but day length increases steadily by a predictable amount.
central_sentences.translation: 在温带地区的春季，每天的温度波动很大，但日长却以可预测的幅度稳定增加。

target_sen.target_sen: Day length is an excellent cue, because it provides a perfectly predictable pattern of change within the year.
Day length is an excellent cue, because it provides a perfectly predictable pattern of change within the year.
In the temperate zone in spring, temperatures fluctuate greatly from day to day, but day length increases steadily by a predictable amount.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“do not respond to light levels”在原文中对应的同义表达是“regardless of photoperiod”，意思都是指不受光周期影响。原文中明确指出这种植物被称为“day-neutral plants”，因此答案是“day-neutral”或“day-neutral plants”。

central_sentences.sentence: Plants which flower after a period of vegetative growth, regardless of photoperiod, are known as day-neutral plants.
central_sentences.translation: 在经历一段营养生长期后开花的植物，不受光周期影响，被称为日中性植物。

target_sen.target_sen: Plants which flower after a period of vegetative growth, regardless of photoperiod, are known as day-neutral plants.

locating_words.locating_word: do not respond to light levels

questions.answer: food / food resources / adequate food / adequate food resources
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "longer days" 对应文章中的 "increasing day lengths"，"nesting" 对应 "begin the nesting cycle"，"availability of" 对应 "will be assured"。通过这些同义替换词，我们可以确定答案句中的 "adequate food resources" 是题干中所问的内容。因此，正确答案是 "food/food resources/adequate food/adequate food resources"。

central_sentences.sentence: Thus many temperate-zone birds use the increasing day lengths in spring as a cue to begin the nesting cycle, because this is a point when adequate food resources will be assured.
central_sentences.translation: 因此，许多温带地区的鸟类利用春季日照时间的增加作为开始筑巢周期的信号，因为这个时候充足的食物资源将得到保证。

target_sen.target_sen: Thus many temperate-zone birds use the increasing day lengths in spring as a cue to begin the nesting cycle, because this is a point when adequate food resources will be assured.
Thus many temperate-zone birds use the increasing day lengths in spring as a cue to begin the nesting cycle, because this is a point when adequate food resources will be assured.
Thus many temperate-zone birds use the increasing day lengths in spring as a cue to begin the nesting cycle, because this is a point when adequate food resources will be assured.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Plants that flower when days are long" 对应原文中的 "Long-day plants"，"depend on" 对应 "require"，"help them reproduce" 对应 "fertilization by insects"。通过这些同义表达的对应关系，我们可以确定答案在 "fertilization by insects" 这部分。原文明确指出长日植物需要昆虫授粉来帮助繁殖，因此答案是 "insects" 或 "fertilization by insects"。

central_sentences.sentence: Long-day plants are adapted for situations that require fertilization by insects, or a long period of seed ripening.
central_sentences.translation: 长日照植物适应需要昆虫授粉或长期种子成熟的情况。

target_sen.target_sen: Long-day plants are adapted for situations that require fertilization by insects, or a long period of seed ripening.

locating_words.locating_word: Plants that flower when days are long

target_sen.target_sen: Long-day plants are adapted for situations that require fertilization by insects, or a long period of seed ripening.
Long-day plants are adapted for situations that require fertilization by insects, or a long period of seed ripening.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是"rainfall"或"suitable rainfall"。题干中的线索词是"Desert annuals"和"reproduction"，在原文中可以找到对应的同义替换词"desert annuals"和"germinate, flower and seed"。原文中明确指出沙漠一年生植物在适合的降雨发生时进行生长和繁殖，因此答案是"rainfall"或"suitable rainfall"。学生需要注意的是，虽然文章中提到了日长（day length），但沙漠一年生植物的繁殖信号是降雨，而不是日长。

central_sentences.sentence: For example, desert annuals germinate, flower and seed whenever suitable rainfall occurs, regardless of the day length.
central_sentences.translation: 例如，沙漠一年生植物在适合的降雨发生时就会发芽、开花和结子，而不受日照长短的影响。

target_sen.target_sen: For example, desert annuals germinate, flower and seed whenever suitable rainfall occurs, regardless of the day length.
For example, desert annuals germinate, flower and seed whenever suitable rainfall occurs, regardless of the day length.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是“sugarcane”，因为文章明确提到甘蔗的光合作用率在光强增加时会继续上升，从未达到极限。这与题干的意思完全一致。通过对比题干和文章中的同义表达，学生可以准确地找到答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Some plants reach maximal photosynthesis at one-quarter full sunlight, and others, like sugarcane, never reach a maximum, but continue to increase photosynthesis rate as light intensity rises.
central_sentences.translation: 一些植物在四分之一的全日光下就达到了最大光合作用，而其他植物，如甘蔗，则从未达到最大值，随着光强的增加，其光合作用率会持续增加。

target_sen.target_sen: Some plants reach maximal photosynthesis at one-quarter full sunlight, and others, like sugarcane, never reach a maximum, but continue to increase photosynthesis rate as light intensity rises.
Some plants reach maximal photosynthesis at one-quarter full sunlight, and others, like sugarcane, never reach a maximum, but continue to increase photosynthesis rate as light intensity rises.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Tolerance to shade" 对应原文中的 "shade-tolerant"，"criterion" 对应 "divided into two groups"，"forestry and horticulture" 在原文中直接出现。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定答案句为 "Plants in general can be divided into two groups: shade-tolerant species and shade-intolerant species. This classification is commonly used in forestry and horticulture."。因此，正确答案是 "classification"，因为它表示分类标准。

central_sentences.sentence: Plants in general can be divided into two groups: shade-tolerant species and shade-intolerant species. This classification is commonly used in forestry and horticulture.
central_sentences.translation: 一般来说，植物可以被分为两组：耐荫种和不耐荫种。这种分类在林业和园艺中常被使用。

target_sen.target_sen: Plants in general can be divided into two groups: shade-tolerant species and shade-intolerant species.
This classification is commonly used in forestry and horticulture.
